Implements Consistent Overhead Byte Stuffing (COBS) encoding functions and related macros.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that provides an implementation of Consistent Overhead Byte Stuffing (COBS), a data encoding scheme. It includes macro definitions and a function prototype to facilitate COBS encoding. The macro `COBS_OVERHEAD(n)` calculates the offset required for in-place encoding, while `MAX_SIZE_AFTER_COBS_ENCODING(n)` determines the maximum buffer size needed to store `n` bytes of data after encoding. The function [`cobs_encode`](<#cobs_encode>) is declared to encode a source buffer (`src`) into a destination buffer (`dst`), which must be large enough to accommodate the encoded data as specified by the `MAX_SIZE_AFTER_COBS_ENCODING` macro. The file includes necessary headers for standard data types and uses `#pragma once` to prevent multiple inclusions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stddef.h`
- `stdint.h`
